mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, PKC, KIT, and VEGFR signaling to block leukemic cell proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; FDA‑approved for FLT3‑mutated AML; MOLM‑13 is a standard sensitive FLT3‑ITD model; expected IC50 in low‑ to mid‑nanomolar range

mechanism: Inhibits RAF, VEGFR, PDGFR, and FLT3 kinases to suppress survival and proliferation pathways
additional_notes: Approved for hepatocellular carcinoma and renal cell carcinoma; strong preclinical data in FLT3‑ITD AML including MOLM‑13; typical IC50 in low‑hundreds of nM

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, FLT3, KIT, FGFR and other tyrosine kinases, blocking oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML and Ph+ ALL; potent against FLT3‑ITD; MOLM‑13 often shows IC50 in low‑nanomolar range

mechanism: Blocks Src and ABL family kinases and also inhibits KIT and some FLT3 signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML and ALL; reported anti‑AML activity; MOLM‑13 viability often reduced in 10–100 nM range

mechanism: Inhibits VEGFR, FGFR, and PDGFR to interfere with angiogenic and growth factor signaling
additional_notes: Approved for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis and NSCLC; shown to impair AML blast proliferation and angiocrine support; AML IC50 typically sub‑ to low‑µM

mechanism: Selectively inhibits anti‑apoptotic protein BCL‑2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; approved in AML combinations and in CLL; MOLM‑13 often highly sensitive with IC50 ≈ 1–50 nM

mechanism: Inhibits BCL‑2, BCL‑XL, and BCL‑W to trigger apoptosis in malignant cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; clinical‑stage molecule; AML IC50 typically tens of nM to low‑µM

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, blocking cytokine‑mediated JAK/STAT signaling
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis, polycythemia vera, and GVHD; synergistic with FLT3/BCL2 inhibitors in AML; IC50 often mid‑nM to low‑µM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton’s tyrosine kinase and also targets ITK, TEC, and EGFR off‑target
additional_notes: FDA‑approved for multiple B‑cell malignancies; limited but positive AML data; expected AML IC50 in high‑nM to µM range

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase leading to oxidative stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Oral gold complex approved for rheumatoid arthritis; preclinical AML data show apoptosis induction; AML IC50 commonly ~0.1–2 µM

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, in complex with copper, interferes with proteasome and NPL4 function
additional_notes: Alcoholism treatment; more potent with Cu2+ (typically 0.5–2 µM disulfiram plus 0.5–2 µM CuCl2 at ~1:1–1:2 molar ratio); robust AML literature; MOLM‑13 usually sensitive in low‑µM range

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I, altering cellular energy metabolism
additional_notes: Widely used diabetes drug; several AML lines respond at 1–10 mM; used as metabolic stressor or combination agent in AML studies

mechanism: Uncouples oxidative phosphorylation and modulates Wnt, STAT3, and mTOR signaling pathways
additional_notes: Anti‑helminthic drug; reported anti‑AML activity via Wnt and mitochondrial disruption; IC50 usually sub‑ to low‑µM

mechanism: Inhibits tubulin polymerization and can modulate hedgehog signaling
additional_notes: Anti‑helminthic; broad anti‑cancer data; AML lines generally respond around 1–10 µM

mechanism: Generates reactive oxygen species and perturbs cellular membranes via redox cycling
additional_notes: Leprosy drug; induces apoptosis and inhibits proliferation in leukemia models; expected AML IC50 in sub‑ to low‑µM range

mechanism: Lysosomotropic weak base that elevates lysosomal pH and inhibits autophagic flux
additional_notes: Antimalarial and anti‑rheumatic; often used to block autophagy in AML; single‑agent IC50 usually 10–30 µM; text also mentions hydroxychloroquine as an alternative in same class

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of ubiquitinated proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma; strong anti‑AML effect; AML and MOLM‑13 IC50 typically ~5–50 nM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the chymotrypsin‑like activity of the 20S proteasome core
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; potent in AML preclinical models; IC50 usually in low‑ to mid‑nanomolar range

mechanism: Pan‑HDAC inhibition leading to altered chromatin structure and transcription, promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; strong pro‑apoptotic effect in AML; typical IC50 5–100 nM in AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits class I and II HDACs, causing histone acetylation and altered gene expression leading to apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved for cutaneous T‑cell lymphoma (CTCL); extensively tested in AML; IC50 usually ~0.5–5 µM in cell lines and can be lower in combinations
